original_abstract:
Integrated Sensing and Communication (ISAC) offers a paradigm shift in wireless system design by enabling efficient spectrum reuse and reduced hardware overhead. This technology is poised to become a cornerstone of 5G-Advanced (5G-A) and 6G networks, supporting a new generation of intelligent applications that demand high-speed data transmission alongside precision environmental sensing. At the heart of this integration is the ISAC signal, the characteristics of which dictate the ultimate performance of both functionalities. 

This paper provides a comprehensive review of ISAC signals through the lens of modern mobile communication systems, categorized into signal design, processing algorithms, and optimization strategies. We first examine signal design frameworks developed for 5G, 5G-A, and 6G architectures. Next, we analyze the radar processing techniques used to extract sensing data from these signals, specifically highlighting the channel information matrix, spectrum lines estimation, and super-resolution methods. Finally, we explore critical optimization domains, including peak-to-average power ratio (PAPR) reduction, interference mitigation, and adaptive signal configuration. By synthesizing these developments, this review aims to establish theoretical and practical benchmarks for the ongoing evolution of ISAC in future cellular networks.
humanized_abstract:
Integrated Sensing and Communication (ISAC) has the potential to transform wireless systems with more efficient spectrum reuse and hardware reduction. ISAC is expected to be a key component in future 5G-Advanced (5G-A) and 6G systems, which are designed to support the proliferation of new intelligent use cases requiring high-speed communication and accurate environmental sensing. All these can be realized by designing the right characteristics of the ISAC signal.

ISAC signals in the context of modern mobile communication systems have attracted a tremendous amount of attention and in this paper, the signal design, processing algorithms and optimization methods are all highlighted. The signal design has attracted a lot of research activities with new designs emerging for 5G, 5G-A, and 6G systems. Moreover, a large amount of research activities focused on the radar processing of the ISAC signals for obtaining the extracted sensing information such as channel information matrix, spectrum lines estimation and super-resolution methods. Finally, other optimization issues such as PAPR reduction, interference mitigation and design of adaptive signal structures have drawn the attentions of many researchers. By summary, this paper discusses theoretical and practical performance benchmarks for ISAC technologies in the context of future cellular networks.
